title: unblock ingest + clean up pending JSONs
description:
## Summary

- The `tracking-import` CronJob has been silently failing for every PR since the `features` table was created without the `UNIQUE` constraint on `pr_number` that `init.sql` advertises. `ON CONFLICT (pr_number)` errored, so 493..528 (36 PRs) never made it into `bachelorprojekt.features`.
- Five PRs (501, 509, 518, 520, 521) further hit the `requirement_id` FK because their bodies cite `FA-*`/`SA-*` IDs and `bachelorprojekt.requirements` is empty in prod.

## What this PR does

- `deploy/tracking/init.sql`: idempotent `ALTER TABLE … ADD CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS` block self-heals existing DBs that predate the unique constraint (no-op on fresh DBs).
- `scripts/track-pr.mjs`: `writeRowToDb` now demotes a parsed `requirement_id` to `NULL` when no matching row exists in `bachelorprojekt.requirements`. The PR still lands in the timeline; it just doesn't link to the requirement.
- `tracking/pending/{493..528}.json`: deleted — all 36 rows were re-ingested into `bachelorprojekt.features` on mentolder today.

## Already applied to live mentolder (out of band)

- `ALTER TABLE bachelorprojekt.features ADD CONSTRAINT features_pr_number_key UNIQUE (pr_number);`
- The 5 FK-blocked rows were inserted directly with `requirement_id = NULL`.
- `SELECT count(*) FROM bachelorprojekt.features WHERE pr_number BETWEEN 493 AND 528;` → 36

The init.sql change is here so any other DB that was created from this schema before the constraint was added (or a fresh restore from backup) heals itself the next time `init.sql` runs.

## Test plan

- [x] Verify `SELECT pr_number FROM bachelorprojekt.features WHERE pr_number BETWEEN 493 AND 528` returns all 36 on mentolder.
- [ ] Next merged PR triggers `tracking-import` cron and lands in `features` automatically (verifies the schema fix end-to-end).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
